source: plan/bearings.md § Article hero art — colorful keyboard SVGs
template: directive: simple line drawing + single splash of color illustrative of subject
subject: Cherry MX2A switch cross-section, side view — top housing with stem hole, cruciform-mount POM stem column drawn in cherry-red splash, 5-coil spring, bent-leaf contact, and bottom housing with pin contacts; a thin dashed callout traces from the stem-housing interface to a magnified circular inset showing the new factory grease ring with scattered lube dots
prompt: Mirror the Gateron Oil King deep-dive's visual rhythm: warm-grey 2px stroke, single coherent cross-section reading as one diagram with subdued didactic ticks. The composition occupies ~85% of canvas width and ~75% of height. The stem column is the focal 'what changed' element so the cherry-red splash lands there (cruciform mount + body fill with a translucent wash, plus a same-hue centerline). The MX2A revision's claim-to-fame is factory lube on the stem-rail interface, so the top-right magnified inset isolates that grease ring at 1:1 attention — the inset is itself a small cherry-red disc with scattered dots, making the article's thesis legible at thumbnail size. Housing halves and the leaf contact stay in stroke-only treatment, so the eye is led from the colored stem through the seam line to the colored inset.
